title: Writing a proposal
explanation:
Writing a proposal

Proposals persuade decision-makers by defining a problem, objectives, method and expected outcomes.

questions.text: [*This proposal outlines a pilot programme to improve digital literacy among older adults.|Proposal outlines pilot programme digital literacy older adults.|Pilot proposal literacy digital adults.]
[*The primary objective is to reduce social isolation through structured training.|Primary objective reduce social isolation structured training.|Objective primary isolation reduce.]
[*The project will be evaluated using attendance records and participant surveys.|Project evaluated attendance records participant surveys.|Evaluated project attendance surveys.]
[*We request funding of 18,000 euros for the initial six-month phase.|Request funding 18,000 euros initial six-month phase.|Funding request euros 18,000.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A proposal should identify a clear ____.  | *problem | joke | colour
Objectives should be measurable and ____.  | *realistic | secret | musical
Include a brief timeline and ____.  | *budget | poem | menu
The tone should remain professional and ____.  | *persuasive | aggressive | casual
[*If approved, the programme could be expanded to neighbouring districts.|Approved programme expanded neighbouring districts.|Programme approved expanded districts.]
[*The need for action is supported by recent community surveys.|Need action supported community surveys recent.|Action need surveys supported.]
[*We would be happy to provide further details upon request.|Happy provide further details upon request.|Details happy provide request.]
[*Thank you for considering this proposal.|Thank considering proposal this.|Proposal thank considering.]
